AddOrganizationMemberRequest.description: AddOrganizationMemberRequest is the request type for adding a new member to an organization.

role.description: The built-in role assigned to the new member. Must be `member`, `admin`, or `billingManager`.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: OrganizationRole is an enum defining a member's role within an organization.
enumFieldComments: OrganizationRoleNone describes the role of non-members.
OrganizationRoleMember is the role for regular members.
OrganizationRoleAdmin is the role for admins.

OrganizationRolePotentialMember is the role for users who are explicitly
not a member of the organization, but are a member of the backing org on
the 3rd party identity provider. (i.e. they could join the org.)

OrganizationRoleStackCollaborator is the role for users who are not a member
of an organization, but have been explicitly granted access to some of the
organization's stacks. So they have limited permissions to access the org's
data.
OrganizationRoleBillingManager is the role for billing admins.

AppConfigValue.description: ConfigValue describes a single (possibly secret) configuration value.

object.description: Object is true if this value is a JSON encoded object.

secret.description: Secret is true if this value is a secret and false otherwise.

string.description:
When Object is false: String is either the plaintext value (for non-secrets) or the base64-encoded ciphertext
(for secrets). When Object is true: String is a JSON encoded object. If both Object and Secret are true, then the
object contains at least one secure value. Secure values in an object are encoded as `{"secure":"ciphertext"}`
where ciphertext is the base64-encoded ciphertext.

AppCreatePolicyPackRequest.description:
CreatePolicyPackRequest defines the request body for creating a new Policy
Pack for an organization. The request contains the metadata related to the
Policy Pack.

description.description: A brief description of the policy pack.

displayName.description: A pretty name for the Policy Pack that is supplied by the package.

metadata.description:
Metadata contains optional data about the environment performing the publish operation,
e.g. the current source code control commit information.

name.description:
Name is a unique URL-safe identifier (at the org level) for the package.
If the name has already been used by the organization, then the request will
create a new version of the Policy Pack (incremented by one). This is supplied
by the CLI.

policies.description:
The Policies outline the specific Policies in the package, and are derived
from the package by the CLI.

provider.description: The cloud provider/platform this policy pack is associated with, e.g. AWS, Azure, etc.

readme.description: README text about the policy pack.

repository.description: A URL to the repository where the policy pack is defined.

versionTag.description:
VersionTag is the semantic version of the Policy Pack. One a version is published, it
cannot never be republished. Older clients will not have a version tag.

AppCreatePolicyPackResponse.description:
CreatePolicyPackResponse is the response from creating a Policy Pack. It returns
a URI to upload the Policy Pack zip file to.

requiredHeaders.description: RequiredHeaders represents headers that the CLI must set in order for the upload to succeed.

version.description: The version number

AppDeploymentV3.description:
DeploymentV3 is the third version of the Deployment. It contains newer versions of the
Resource and Operation API types and a placeholder for a stack's secrets configuration.
Note that both deployment schema versions 3 and 4 can be unmarshaled into DeploymentV3.

manifest.description: Manifest contains metadata about this deployment.

pending_operations.description: PendingOperations are all operations that were known by the engine to be currently executing.

resources.description: Resources contains all resources that are currently part of this stack after this deployment has finished.

DiagnosticEvent is emitted whenever a diagnostic message is provided, for example errors from
a cloud resource provider while trying to create or update a resource.

message.description: The message content

AppEncryptValueRequest.description: EncryptValueRequest defines the request body for encrypting a value.

AppEncryptValueResponse.description: EncryptValueResponse defines the response body for an encrypted value.

AppEngineEvent.description:
EngineEvent describes a Pulumi engine event, such as a change to a resource or diagnostic
message. EngineEvent is a discriminated union of all possible event types, and exactly one
field will be non-nil.

policyAnalyzeStackSummaryEvent.description: The policy analyze stack summary event

sequence.description:
Sequence is a unique, and monotonically increasing number for each engine event sent to the
Pulumi Service. Since events may be sent concurrently, and/or delayed via network routing,
the sequence number is to ensure events can be placed into a total ordering.

- No two events can have the same sequence number.
- Events with a lower sequence number must have been emitted before those with a higher
  sequence number.

timestamp.description: Timestamp is a Unix timestamp (seconds) of when the event was emitted.

AppEngineEventBatch.description: EngineEventBatch is a group of engine events.

AppErrorEvent.description:
ErrorEvent is emitted when an internal error occurs in the engine. This is not meant
to be used for user facing errors, but rather for internal errors, where an event
can help with debugging.

AppGetDefaultOrganizationResponse.description:
GetDefaultOrganizationResponse returns the backend's opinion of which organization
to default to for a given user, if a default organization has not been configured.

GitHubLogin.description: Returns the organization name. Can be an empty string, if the user is a member of no organizations

Messages.description:
Messages is a list of messages that should be displayed to the user that contextualize
the default org; for example: warning new users if their default org as returned by the
service is on an expiring trial and not free tier, with possible recommendations
on how to configure their default org locally.
Can be possibly empty.

AppPatchUpdateCheckpointDeltaRequest.description:
PatchUpdateCheckpointDeltaRequest defines the body of a request to the bandwidth-optimized version of the patch
update checkpoint endpoint of the service API. It is semantically equivalent to the PatchUpdateCheckpointRequest, but
instead of transferring the entire Deployment as a JSON blob, it encodes it as a textual diff against the last-saved
deployment. This conserves bandwidth on large resources.

checkpointHash.description: SHA256 hash of the result of aplying the DeploymentDelta to the previously saved deployment.

deploymentDelta.description: Textual diff that recovers the desired deployment JSON when applied to the previously saved deployment JSON.

sequenceNumber.description: Idempotency key incremented by the client on every PATCH call within the same update.

version.description: Protocol version.

AppPatchUpdateCheckpointRequest.description:
PatchUpdateCheckpointRequest defines the body of a request to the patch update checkpoint endpoint of the service
API. The `Deployment` field is expected to contain a serialized `Deployment` value, the schema of which is indicated
by the `Version` field.

version.description: The version number

AppPatchUpdateVerbatimCheckpointRequest.description:
PatchUpdateVerbatimCheckpointRequest defines the body of a request to a patch update checkpoint endpoint of the
service API, where `UntypedDeloyment` is an `apitype.UntypedDeployment`. Whereas the PatchUpdateCheckpointRequest
API is subject to the service reformatting how a checkpoint is persisted, PatchUpdateVerbatimCheckpointRequest
enables the CLI to define the exact format.
Designed to be compatible with the PatchUpdateCheckpointDeltaRequest API, where formatting is critical in calculating
textual diffs.

AppResourceV3.description:
ResourceV3 is the third version of the Resource API type. It absorbs a few breaking changes:
1. It adds a map from input property names to the dependencies that affect that input property. This is used to
improve the precision of delete-before-create operations.
2. It adds a new boolean field, `PendingReplacement`, that marks resources that have been deleted as part of a
delete-before-create operation but have not yet been recreated.
Migrating from ResourceV2 to ResourceV3 involves:
1. Populating the map from input property names to dependencies by assuming that every dependency listed in
`Dependencies` affects every input property.

AppStepEventMetadata.description:
StepEventMetadata describes a "step" within the Pulumi engine, which is any concrete action
to migrate a set of cloud resources from one state to another.

detailedDiff.description: The diff for this step as a map of property paths to difference types. An empty map indicates no detailed property-level diff is available; if this field is omitted or null, the diff was not computed.

diffs.description: Keys that changed with this step.

keys.description: Keys causing a replacement (only applicable for `create` and `replace` Ops).

logical.description: Logical is set if the step is a logical operation in the program.

new.description: New is the state of the resource after performing the step.

old.description: Old is the state of the resource before performing the step.

op.description: Op is the operation being performed.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments:
OpType describes the type of operation performed to a resource managed by Pulumi.
Should generally mirror deploy.StepOp, but we clone it in this package to add
flexibility in case there is a breaking change in the backend-type.
enumFieldComments: OpSame indicates no change was made.
OpCreate indicates a new resource was created.
OpUpdate indicates an existing resource was updated.
OpDelete indicates an existing resource was deleted.
OpReplace indicates an existing resource was replaced with a new one.
OpCreateReplacement indicates a new resource was created for a replacement.
OpDeleteReplaced indicates an existing resource was deleted after replacement.
OpRead indicates reading an existing resource.
OpReadReplacement indicates reading an existing resource for a replacement.
OpRefresh indicates refreshing an existing resource.
OpReadDiscard indicates removing a resource that was read.
OpDiscardReplaced indicates discarding a read resource that was replaced.
OpRemovePendingReplace indicates removing a pending replace resource.
OpImport indicates importing an existing resource.
OpImportReplacement indicates replacement of an existing resource with an imported resource.

A value of nil means that no new updates will be available. Everything has been returned to
the client and the update has completed.

ConsumptionUsage.description: ConsumptionUsage captures the unit rate and current consumption for a consumption-based product.

statsV0.description: StatsV0 are the usage statistics, and will be populated for graduated-v0 plans.

statsV1.description: StatsV1 are the usage statistics, and will be populated for graduated-v1 plans.

unitRateGraduatedV0.description: UnitRateGraduatedV0 will be populated for `UnitRateSchemaGraduatedV0` plans.

unitRateGraduatedV1.description: UnitRateGraduatedV1 will be populated for `UnitRateSchemaGraduatedV1` plans.

unitRateGraduatedV2.description: UnitRateGraduatedV2 will be populated for `UnitRateSchemaGraduatedV2` plans.

unitRateSchema.description:
UnitRateSchema captures which pricing plan is associated with the product subscription,
and further indicates which pricing rate structure should be read.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: UnitRateSchema describes the pricing schema used for a subscription's unit rate.
enumFieldComments:
UnitRateSchemaGraduatedV0 is the initial pricing schema that we launched with the advent of our
Growth plans. Pricing plan rates are pasred into `UnitRateGraduatedV0`.

UnitRateSchemaGraduated plans used graduated pricing plans, with plans parsed into
`UnitRateGraduatedV1`. These plans are introduced starting 2025.

UnitRateSchemaGraduatedV2 is used for pricing plans where the free tier is handled
via credit grants (dollar credits) rather than being baked into the pricing tiers. Rates are parsed into
`UnitRateGraduatedV2` and contain only paid (on-demand) tiers. The tiers field uses `UnitRateGraduatedTier`
for graduated prices with multiple tiers, and a single-element tiers array for per-unit prices with only
one rate tier.

ConsumptionUsageStatsV0.description: ConsumptionUsageStatsV0 are the usage statistics for a billing family's total usage for graduated-v0 plans.

estimatedOnDemandCost.description: EstimatedOnDemandCost is the estimated cost of the on-demand usage (beyond the free units), in USD cents.

estimatedTotalCost.description: EstimatedTotalCost is the estimated total cost of billing family's usage for the current billing cycle, in USD cents.

includedUnits.description: IncludedUnits is the number of units included in the subscription.

isIncludedUnitsFree.description: IsIncludedUnitsFree indicates whether the included units are free of charge.

onDemandRate.description: OnDemandRate is the on-demand rate for the billing family's total usage, in USD cents per unit.

allOf.description:
ConsumptionUsageStatsV1 are the usage statistics for a billing family's total usage for graduated-v1 plans.
It extends ConsumptionUsageStatsV0 with additional fields for effective rate and volume savings.

effectiveRate.description: EffectiveRate is the effective rate for the billing family's total usage, in USD dollar per unit.

volumeSavingsPercentage.description: VolumeSavingsAmount is the amount of volume savings for the billing family's total usage, in USD dollar.

DeploymentSourceGit.description:
DeploymentSourceGit is like SourceContextGit but does not include the Auth data as we don't want to include that

commit.description:
(optional) Commit is the hash of the commit to deploy. If used, HEAD will be in detached mode. This
is mutually exclusive with the Branch setting. Either value needs to be specified.

DeploymentSourceTemplate.description:
DeploymentSourceTemplate is like SourceContextTemplate but does not include the Auth data as we don't want to
include that in the deployment response.

sourceUrl.description:
The URL of the template source. Supports two URL schemes:

**Registry-backed templates** use the `registry://` scheme with the format:
`registry://templates/source/publisher/name[@version]`

- `source`: The template source identifier (e.g., the registry source name)
- `publisher`: The organization or user that published the template
- `name`: The template name
- `version`: Optional semver version (e.g., `1.0.0`). If omitted, defaults to the latest version

Example: `registry://templates/pulumi/acme-corp/aws-vpc@2.1.0`

**VCS-backed templates** use standard VCS URLs (GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, etc.):
`https://github.com/org/repo`

GetUpdateEventsResponse.description: GetUpdateEventsResponse contains the engine events for am update.

continuationToken.description:
ContinuationToken is an opaque value the client can send to fetch more recent
events if the update is still in progress. Will be nil once all events have
been returned and the update is complete.

events.description:
Events are returned sorted by their internal sequence number (not exposed to the API).
So the last Event in the slice is the most recent event which was stored in the database.
(Should sort identical to timestamp, but may not if we support parallel writes.)

GetUpdateTimelineResponse.description: GetUpdateTimelineResponse is the response type returning the timeline for a given update.

collatedPullRequest.description: CollatedPullRequest is the source pull request that was merged, which triggered the stack's update.

collatedUpdateEvents.description:
CollatedUpdateEvents is the set of update events that are "relevant" to the update.
It will contain the sequences of previews that were performed in the same "group",
e.g. a GitHub PR, that lead to the final update.

If the sequence of previews happened spanned when the stack was updated, e.g. the first
preview was at version N - 1 and the last preview was at version N + 1, then the update
to stack version N will be included.

Will be empty if the update couldn't be associated with a particular sequence of previews.

previews.description:
Previews contains all previews for the given stack update that weren't part of the collated
set. For example, from other GitHub pull requests that haven't been merged, previews ran on
developer machines, etc.

update.description: Update is the information about the completed update for the stack.

GitAuthConfig.description:
GitAuthConfig specifies git authentication configuration options.
There are 3 different authentication options:
- Personal access token
- SSH private key (and its optional password)
- Basic auth username and password
Only 1 authentication mode is valid.

ListMemberStackPermissionsResponse.description:
ListMemberStackPermissionsResponse describes all the permissions that have been set
for a user for a specific stack.

explicitPermission.description: The explicitly assigned permission for this user on this stack

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments:
StackPermission is a value describing the permission level a user has for a Pulumi stack.
Like OrganizationRole, there is a total ordering of StackPermission values, where each value
grants an additional layer of permissions. IMPORTANT: Do not rely on the numeric values for
auth checks, instead, just refer to these as opaque names. e.g. use explicit matching in a
case-statements instead of "if perm >= StackPermissionX". This prevents errors if we later
add a new StackPermission "in between" two existing ones, but with a new, higher value.
Previously, the StackPermission value was a series of bit flags, with each bit controlling
a specific operation on the stack. However, that proved difficult to describe and maintain.
So the newer, "fixed set" approach is used instead.
The conversion from the legacy permission values and the new StackPermission values is done
transparently, whenever data is read/written to the database. (Converting to the newer
values as needed.) We will only be able to remove these legacy values after we deploy the
code which can read both formats, and only writes the newer format.
enumFieldComments: StackPermissionNone provides no access.

StackPermissionRead provides read-only access, including the ability to
decrypt encrypted configuration secrets stored in the service.
StackPermissionWrite provides read/write access to a stack.

StackPermissionAdmin provides admin-level access to the stack. For example,
being able to run `pulumi destroy` or delete the stack.

StackPermissionCreator is an implementation quirk of how we mark the user who created
a stack. The value is not exported from this module, and is converted to StackPermissionAdmin
before being returned to callers. However, in a few places we want to handle the user who
created a stack a little differently, and this allows us to do so without exporting a separate
"StackPermissionAdminWhoAlsoHappenedToCreateTheStackToo" value.

organizationDefault.description: The organization's default stack permission level

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments:
StackPermission is a value describing the permission level a user has for a Pulumi stack.
Like OrganizationRole, there is a total ordering of StackPermission values, where each value
grants an additional layer of permissions. IMPORTANT: Do not rely on the numeric values for
auth checks, instead, just refer to these as opaque names. e.g. use explicit matching in a
case-statements instead of "if perm >= StackPermissionX". This prevents errors if we later
add a new StackPermission "in between" two existing ones, but with a new, higher value.
Previously, the StackPermission value was a series of bit flags, with each bit controlling
a specific operation on the stack. However, that proved difficult to describe and maintain.
So the newer, "fixed set" approach is used instead.
The conversion from the legacy permission values and the new StackPermission values is done
transparently, whenever data is read/written to the database. (Converting to the newer
values as needed.) We will only be able to remove these legacy values after we deploy the
code which can read both formats, and only writes the newer format.
enumFieldComments: StackPermissionNone provides no access.

StackPermissionRead provides read-only access, including the ability to
decrypt encrypted configuration secrets stored in the service.
StackPermissionWrite provides read/write access to a stack.

StackPermissionAdmin provides admin-level access to the stack. For example,

StackPermissionCreator is an implementation quirk of how we mark the user who created
a stack. The value is not exported from this module, and is converted to StackPermissionAdmin
before being returned to callers. However, in a few places we want to handle the user who
created a stack a little differently, and this allows us to do so without exporting a separate
"StackPermissionAdminWhoAlsoHappenedToCreateTheStackToo" value.

organizationRole.description: The user's role within the organization

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: OrganizationRole is an enum defining a member's role within an organization.
enumFieldComments: OrganizationRoleNone describes the role of non-members.
OrganizationRoleMember is the role for regular members.
OrganizationRoleAdmin is the role for admins.

OrganizationRolePotentialMember is the role for users who are explicitly
not a member of the organization, but are a member of the backing org on
the 3rd party identity provider. (i.e. they could join the org.)

OrganizationRoleStackCollaborator is the role for users who are not a member
of an organization, but have been explicitly granted access to some of the
organization's stacks. So they have limited permissions to access the org's
data.
OrganizationRoleBillingManager is the role for billing admins.

credentials in the form of an assume-role session.
See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_create_for-idp_oidc.html for details on creating an
appropriately-configured IAM role.

OperationContextAzureOIDCConfiguration.description:
OperationContextAzureOIDCConfiguration contains information about how to exchange an OIDC token for temporary Azure
credentials in the form of a federated workload identity.
See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/workload-identity-federation-create-trust?pivots=identity-wif-apps-methods-azp
for details on how to create an appropriately-configured identity.

OperationContextGCPOIDCConfiguration.description:
OperationContextGCPOIDCConfiguration contains information about how to exchange an OIDC token for temporary GCP
credentials in the form of a service account connected to a workload identity pool.
See https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/configuring-workload-identity-federation for details on how to create an
appropriately-configured workload identity pool and service account.

links.description: Links to the member in the Pulumi Console

role.description: **Deprecated:** Use `fgaRole` instead. The member's built-in role within the organization. For members assigned a custom role, this is the closest built-in projection (`member`, `admin`, or `billingManager`) and may lose detail; `fgaRole` is authoritative.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: OrganizationRole is an enum defining a member's role within an organization.
enumFieldComments: OrganizationRoleNone describes the role of non-members.
OrganizationRoleMember is the role for regular members.
OrganizationRoleAdmin is the role for admins.

OrganizationRolePotentialMember is the role for users who are explicitly
not a member of the organization, but are a member of the backing org on
the 3rd party identity provider. (i.e. they could join the org.)

OrganizationRoleStackCollaborator is the role for users who are not a member
of an organization, but have been explicitly granted access to some of the
organization's stacks. So they have limited permissions to access the org's
data.
OrganizationRoleBillingManager is the role for billing admins.

user.description: The user information for this organization member.

virtualAdmin.description:
VirtualAdmin indicates that the member does not have admin access on the
backing identity provider, but does have admin access to the Pulumi organization.

defaultDeploymentRoleId.description: DefaultDeploymentRoleID is the ID of the default role used for deployments when no specific role is configured in the stack's deployment settings. If unset, deployments run using the triggering user's own permissions.

If unset, defaults to the "Member" role.

defaultStackPermission.description:
DefaultStackPermission is the default permission every member
has for accessing the organization's stacks.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments:
StackPermission is a value describing the permission level a user has for a Pulumi stack.
Like OrganizationRole, there is a total ordering of StackPermission values, where each value
grants an additional layer of permissions. IMPORTANT: Do not rely on the numeric values for
auth checks, instead, just refer to these as opaque names. e.g. use explicit matching in a
case-statements instead of "if perm >= StackPermissionX". This prevents errors if we later
add a new StackPermission "in between" two existing ones, but with a new, higher value.
Previously, the StackPermission value was a series of bit flags, with each bit controlling
a specific operation on the stack. However, that proved difficult to describe and maintain.
So the newer, "fixed set" approach is used instead.
The conversion from the legacy permission values and the new StackPermission values is done
transparently, whenever data is read/written to the database. (Converting to the newer
values as needed.) We will only be able to remove these legacy values after we deploy the
code which can read both formats, and only writes the newer format.
enumFieldComments: StackPermissionNone provides no access.

StackPermissionRead provides read-only access, including the ability to
decrypt encrypted configuration secrets stored in the service.
StackPermissionWrite provides read/write access to a stack.

StackPermissionAdmin provides admin-level access to the stack. For example,
being able to run `pulumi destroy` or delete the stack.

StackPermissionCreator is an implementation quirk of how we mark the user who created
a stack. The value is not exported from this module, and is converted to StackPermissionAdmin
before being returned to callers. However, in a few places we want to handle the user who
created a stack a little differently, and this allows us to do so without exporting a separate
"StackPermissionAdminWhoAlsoHappenedToCreateTheStackToo" value.

PolicyGroup.description: PolicyGroup details the name, applicable entities and the applied PolicyPacks.

accounts.description: List of Insights account names that are members of this policy group.

agentPoolId.description: Agent pool ID for audit policy evaluation. Defaults to Pulumi hosted pool if not specified.

isOrgDefault.description: True if this is either the default stacks or default accounts policy group for the organization.

TotalAccountResources.description: Total number of resources in all accounts

TotalStackResources.description: Total number of resources in all stacks

TotalStacks.description: Total number of stacks in the organization

PolicyIssue.description: PolicyIssue represents a policy violation or issue detected during policy evaluation.

assignedTo.description: The user the policy issue is assigned to.

entityId.description: The identifier of the entity this issue applies to.

entityProject.description: The project name (for stack entities) or parent Insights account name (for resource entities).

entityType.description: The type of entity this issue applies to.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: The type of entity associated with a policy issue. Valid values: 'stack', 'insights-account'.

id.description: The unique identifier of the policy issue.

kind.description: The kind of policy issue (audit or preventative).

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: Whether a policy issue is audit-only or preventative. Audit issues are informational; preventative issues block operations.

lastModified.description: The timestamp when the issue was last modified.

level.description: The enforcement level of the policy (e.g. advisory, mandatory, disabled).

message.description: A human-readable message describing the policy violation.

observedAt.description: The timestamp when the issue was first observed.

policyGroupName.description: The name of the policy group this issue belongs to.

policyGroupType.description: The type of the policy group this issue belongs to.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: PolicyGroupMode represents the enforcement mode for a policy group

policyName.description: The name of the policy that was violated.

policyPack.description: The name of the policy pack that produced this issue.

policyPackTag.description: The tag of the policy pack version that produced this issue.

priority.description: The priority level of the policy issue.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: Priority level of a policy issue, from P0 (most critical) to P4 (least critical).

resourceName.description: The name of the resource that violated the policy.

resourceProvider.description: The provider of the resource that violated the policy.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource that violated the policy.

resourceURN.description: The URN of the resource that violated the policy.

resourceVersion.description: The resource version where the issue was detected.

severity.description: The severity of the policy violation.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: Indicates the severity of a policy.

stackVersion.description: The stack version where the issue was detected.

status.description: The current status of the policy issue.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: Lifecycle status of a policy issue. Valid values: open, in_progress, by_design, fixed, ignored. Note: fixed is a read-only status set automatically when an issue is resolved; it cannot be manually set via the API.

PolicyIssueFilterValue.description: PolicyIssueFilterValue represents a filter option with its count of matching policy issues.

count.description: The count of policy issues matching this filter value.

name.description: The name of the filter value.

PolicyIssueFiltersRequest.description: PolicyIssueFiltersRequest is a request to retrieve available filter values for policy issues.

filterModel.description: Optional filter model to apply when getting available filter values. Same format as used in policy issues list.

PolicyIssueFiltersResponse.description: PolicyIssueFiltersResponse contains the available filter values for a given field in policy issues.

field.description: The field name for which filter values are provided.

values.description: The available filter values for the field.

PolicyResultsMetadata.description: PolicyResultsMetadata returns high level policy compliance statistics for an organization.

resourcesTotalCount.description: Total number of resources covered by policies

PolicyViolationV2.description: PolicyViolationV2 represents a policy violation detected during an update or resource scan.

accountName.description: The Insights account name associated with the violation, for resource-scoped violations.

id.description: The unique identifier of the policy violation.

kind.description: The kind of policy violation (audit or preventative).

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: Whether a policy issue is audit-only or preventative. Audit issues are informational; preventative issues block operations.

level.description: The enforcement level of the violated policy (e.g. advisory, mandatory, disabled).

message.description: A human-readable message describing the policy violation.

observedAt.description: The timestamp when the violation was observed.

policyName.description: The name of the policy that was violated.

policyPack.description: The name of the policy pack that produced this violation.

policyPackTag.description: The tag of the policy pack version that produced this violation.

projectName.description: The name of the project containing the violating resource.

resourceName.description: The name of the resource that violated the policy.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource that violated the policy.

resourceURN.description: The URN of the resource that violated the policy.

resourceVersion.description: The resource version where the violation was detected.

stackName.description: The name of the stack containing the violating resource.

stackVersion.description: The stack version where the violation was detected.

ProviderSchema.description: ProviderSchema describes the input and output schemas for a provider.

description.description: A description of the provider.

inputs.description: The input schema for the provider.

outputs.description: The output schema for the provider.

PublishPackageVersionCompleteRequest.description: PublishPackageVersionCompleteRequest is a request to mark a package version publish operation as complete.

operationID.description: The identifier of the publish operation to complete.

PublishPackageVersionCompleteResponse.description: PublishPackageVersionCompleteResponse is the response after completing a package version publish operation.

PublishTemplateVersionCompleteRequest.description: PublishTemplateVersionCompleteRequest is a request to mark a template version publish operation as complete.

PulumiGitHubOrganization.description: PulumiGitHubOrganization is a union of a GitHub organization with any Pulumi-specific data.

knownToPulumi.description: KnownToPulumi is true if there is a Pulumi organization backed by the GitHub organization.

vcsKind.description: The identity provider associated with this GitHub organization.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments:
IdentityProvider describes an identity system used by the Pulumi Service. It generally maps 1:1
to OrganizationKind, but we intentionally keep this separate (and with different values!) for
compatibility with older code that passes these values between the Node backend, Angular, and
our API backend.

PulumiProject.description:
PulumiProject describes a grouping of Pulumi stacks. It is based on Pulumi.yaml,
but is specific to the Pulumi Service.

description.description: The project description from Pulumi.yaml.

orgName.description: The name of the organization that owns this project.

repoName.description: The repository name this project belongs to.

stacks.description: The stacks belonging to this project.

PulumiProjectSettings.description: PulumiProjectSettings contains the settings for a Pulumi project, including source and destination paths.

addStacksDisabledReason.description:
An optional reason for disabling new stacks.
If `CanAddStacks` is false there may be additional context provided
via this field such that the user can determine why they cannot add
new stacks. For example, the organization has hit their max stack quota
or the project's source and/or destination no longer exist.

canAddStacks.description:
A flag denoting if the user can add stacks to the project.
This flag might be false if the organization has hit their max stack quota
or if the project's source and/or destination no longer exist.

source.description: A path to the source of a project

PulumiRepository.description:
PulumiRepository is a grouping of "Projects". We also return a subset of the organization's
VCS repo with the same name, should it exist.

orgName.description: The name of the organization that owns this repository.

vcsInfo.description: Version control system information for the repository.

PulumiStack.description:
PulumiStack contains a name and some information for the frontend to construct a route to the details page. The
custom routing parameters are here to help land the new identity model in an iterative fashion and long term will
be removed.

driftDetected.description: Whether the last drift detection run found differences between the desired and actual state.

hasDeploymentSettings.description: Whether the stack has Pulumi Deployments settings configured (source, OIDC, etc.).

lastUpdate.description: Summary of the last update to the stack. nil if never updated.

protectedByPolicy.description: If true, the stack has at least one Policy Pack enforced.

routingProject.description: The project name used for routing to the stack details page.

routingRepo.description: The repository name used for routing to the stack details page.

tags.description: Tags associated with the stack as key-value pairs.

allOf.description: A pulumi template remote where the source URL contains a valid Pulumi.yaml file.

sourceName.description: The name of the template source - for display purposes

sourceURL.description: The unique url that identifies the template to the service

RbacPermissionConstraints.description: Constraints that can be applied to an RBAC permission.

MaxOpenDuration.description: If not zero, the amount of time, in seconds, to keep an environment open

RbacScope.description: RbacScope represents a permission and its metadata

name.description: The permission name for this scope.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: RbacPermission enumerates the permissions available in the RBAC system.
enumFieldComments: OrganizationTransferStacks differs from Stack Transfer as it enables transferring all stacks within the organization, rather than being tied to an individual stack.

RbacScopeGroup.description: RbacScopeGroup represents a group of scopes for a resource group

name.description: The name of this scope group.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: RbacScopeGroupName enumerates the names of RBAC scope groups for organizing permissions by resource type.

scopes.description: The list of scopes in this group.

RbacScopeMetadata.description: Metadata associated with an RBAC scope.

description.description: A human-readable description of this scope.

allOf.description: Response containing the details of an Insights account.

RegistryPolicyPack.description:
RegistryPolicyPack represents the core metadata for a policy pack in the registry.
This is the primary data structure returned by most registry API endpoints.

accessLevel.description: AccessLevel is the client's level of access to this policy pack.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: The level of access a client has to a registry policy pack.
enumFieldComments: Full access to a policy pack (view + use)
Access to view a policy pack
No access to a policy pack

displayName.description:
DisplayName is a human-readable name for this policy pack.
This is typically more descriptive than the technical name.

enforcementLevels.description: EnforcementLevels are the client's allowed enforcement levels for this policy pack.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: EnforcementLevel indicates how a policy should be enforced
enumFieldComments:
Advisory is an enforcement level where the resource is still created, but a
message is displayed to the user for informational / warning purposes.
Mandatory is an enforcement level that prevents a resource from being created.
Remediate is an enforcement level that fixes policy issues instead of issuing diagnostics.
Disabled is an enforcement level that disables the policy from being enforced.

id.description:
ID is the unique identifier for this policy pack in the registry.
This is a UUID that corresponds to the policy pack's database ID.

name.description:
Name is the unique identifier for this policy pack within the publisher's namespace.
Policy pack names must be URL-safe and unique per publisher.

publisher.description:
Publisher is the organization or user that published this policy pack.
This corresponds to the Pulumi organization name.

source.description:
Source indicates where this policy pack is hosted (e.g., "private", "pulumi").
Currently, only "private" policy packs are supported.

version.description:
Version is the semantic version of this policy pack.
This represents the latest or specific version being referenced.

RemoveEnvironmentIdentifier.description: Identifier used when removing an environment from a team.

envName.description: The name of the environment to remove.

projectName.description: The name of the project containing the environment.

RemoveStackIdentifier.description: RemoveStackIdentifier is the identifier used in the UpdateTeamRequest when removing a stack from a team.

projectName.description: The name of the project containing the stack.

stackName.description: The name of the stack to remove.

Resource.description:
Resource is the shape of a Pulumi-managed resource as exposed from the Pulumi Service.
This type wraps pulumi/pulumi's pkg/apitype ResourceV1 and ResourceV2 types, which are
sent from the CLI to the Service as part of performing updates. Those types wrap
pulumi/pulumi's pkg/resource State type, which is the engine-internal representation.

additionalSecretOutputs.description: AdditionalSecretOutputs is a list of outputs that were explicitly marked as secret when the resource was created.

aliases.description: Aliases is a list of previous URNs that this resource may have had in previous deployments.

custom.description: Custom is true when the resource is managed by a plugin.

delete.description: Delete is true when the resource should be deleted during the next update.

deletedWith.description:
If set, the providers Delete method will not be called for this resource
if specified resource is being deleted as well.

dependencies.description: Dependencies contains the URN dependency edges to other resources that this depends on.

external.description: External is set to true when the lifecycle of this resource is not managed by Pulumi.

id.description: ID is the provider-assigned resource ID, if any, for custom resources.

initErrors.description:
InitErrors is the set of errors encountered in the process of initializing resource (i.e.,
during create or update).

inputs.description: Inputs are the input properties supplied to the provider.

outputs.description: Outputs are the output properties returned by the provider after provisioning.

parent.description: Parent is an optional parent URN if this resource is a child of it.

protect.description: Protect is set to true when this resource is "protected" and may not be deleted.

providerInputs.description:
The inputs to the provider which created this resource. This can be used when generating console links
(e.g. the value of aws:region will be in this property bag with the key "region" and the value of whatever
region was set on the provider that created this resource).

retainOnDelete.description:
If set to True, the providers Delete method will not be called for this resource.
Pulumi simply stops tracking the deleted resource.

type.description: Type is the resource's full type token.

urn.description: URN uniquely identifying this resource within a stack. (But not globally across all stacks.)

ResourceCountSummary.description:
ResourceCountSummary represents a single point of summary for resources
under management for an organization.

day.description: The day of month. Ranges from 1 to 31.

hour.description: The hour of the day. Ranges from 0 to 23.

month.description: The month of the year. Ranges from 1 to 12.

resourceHours.description:
The RHUM, which is the number of hours the resources under management have
been running. Calculated by getting the sum of all the resources for the
given time frame. 1 resource hour = 1 Pulumi credit.

resources.description:
The RUM (total number of resources under management at a given time).
Calculated by getting the average of the all the resources for the given
time frame.

weekNumber.description: The week number in the year with Sunday marking the start of the week. Ranges from 0-53.

ResourceDependency.description: A single dependency in a chain of dependencies used to list a resource.

id.description: The resource identifier.

ResourceInfo.description: ResourceInfo wraps a basic resource and provides contextual information.

resource.description: The underlying resource with its properties, URN, type, and provider state.

ResourceMigrationAnnotation.description: A user-created migration annotation on a discovered resource.

linkedResourceUrn.description: URN of the resource this one was migrated as part of.

note.description: Free-text note about this resource's migration status. May be empty when only a status override is set.

statusOverride.description: User override of the computed migration status. Only Migrated and NotApplicable are valid overrides.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: Migration status of a discovered resource relative to a comparison Pulumi stack. Migrated and PulumiOnly are only possible when the compareTo parameter is provided.
enumFieldComments: Resource identity match found in the comparison stack.
Resource exists only in the comparison Pulumi stack.
Mapped to a Pulumi type with virtual state available.
Mapped to a Pulumi type but no virtual state yet.
No Pulumi type mapping exists for this resource.
Migration status is not applicable (e.g. stack or deployment containers).

ResourceResult.description:
ResourceResult is the user-facing type for our indexed resources.
If you add a property here, don't forget to update fieldMappings to make it
queryable!

account.description: The Insights account name that discovered or manages this resource.

created.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the resource was first indexed.

custom.description: Whether this is a custom resource managed by a provider plugin.

delete.description: Whether this resource is pending deletion.

dependencies.description: URNs of resources that this resource depends on.

dependents.description: URNs of resources that depend on this resource.

external.description: Whether the lifecycle of this resource is not managed by Pulumi.

fingerprint.description: A fingerprint uniquely identifying this resource's state.

id.description: The provider-assigned resource ID.

managed.description: Whether this resource is managed by Pulumi IaC stacks or discovered by Insights scanning. One of 'managed' or 'discovered'.

matches.description: Matched search terms mapped to their highlighted values.

metadata.description: Additional metadata associated with the resource.

modified.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the resource was last updated in the index.

module.description: The module that contains this resource.

package.description: The package that provides this resource.

parent_urn.description: The URN of the parent resource, if any.

pending.description: The pending operation on this resource, if any (e.g. creating, updating, deleting).

project.description: The project that contains this resource.

properties.description: The resource's input/output properties as a JSON object. Only populated when explicitly requested.

protected.description: Whether this resource is protected from deletion.

provider_urn.description: The URN of the provider for this resource.

sourceCount.description: The number of sources for this resource.

stack.description: The stack that contains this resource.

teams.description: The teams that have access to this resource.

type.description: The full type token of the resource (e.g. aws:s3/bucket:Bucket).

urn.description: The URN uniquely identifying this resource within a stack.

ResourceSearchPagination.description: ResourceSearchPagination provides links for paging through results.

cursor.description: An opaque cursor for resuming pagination.

next.description: Link to the next page of results.

previous.description: Link to the previous page of results.

ResourceSearchResult.description: ResourceSearchResult is a collected of resource search results.

pagination.description: Pagination links for navigating through results.

resources.description: The list of matching resource results.

total.description: The total number of matching resources.

ResponseAuditLogs.description:
ResponseAuditLogs represents the audit event logs response given
when listing audit log events. Contains a continuation token for
pagination as well as a list of the AuditLogEvent objects.

auditLogEvents.description: The list of audit log events.

continuationToken.description: A continuation token for paginating through audit log results.

RestoreDeletedStackRequest.description: Request to restore a previously deleted stack.

stackName.description: The name of the stack to restore.

allOf.description: Request to restore a previously deleted environment.

deletionTimestamp.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp of the environment's deletion, used to identify which deleted version to restore.

RetractEnvironmentRevisionRequest.description: Request to retract a specific revision of an environment.

reason.description: The reason for retracting the revision.

replacement.description: The revision number to use as a replacement, if any.

RotateEnvironmentRequest.description: Request to rotate secrets in an environment.

paths.description: The paths of the secrets to rotate.

RotateEnvironmentResponse.description: Response from a secret rotation operation on an environment.

diagnostics.description: Diagnostics generated during the rotation operation.

id.description: The unique identifier for this rotation operation.

secretRotationEvent.description: Details of the secret rotation event.

SAMLOrganization.description: SAMLOrganization is a Pulumi organization combined with its SAML-specific data.

entityId.description:
The following fields can be empty if the metadata (IDPSSODescriptor)
itself is empty for the organization.

idpSsoDescriptor.description: The SAML Identity Provider SSO descriptor XML.

nameIdFormat.description: The SAML NameID format used by the identity provider.

ssoUrl.description: The SSO URL for the identity provider.

validUntil.description:
ValidUntil is the date until which the X.509 Certificate
issued to the organization by the identity provider is valid.

validationError.description: ValidationError will be set only if there is an error validating the SAML org's metadata XML.

SSHAuth.description:
SSHAuth configures ssh-based auth for git authentication.
SSHPrivateKey is required but password is optional.

password.description: An optional password or passphrase for the SSH private key.

sshPrivateKey.description: The SSH private key used for authentication.

SSHAuthRequest.description: Request to configure SSH-based authentication for git operations.

password.description: An optional password for the SSH private key.

sshPrivateKey.description: The SSH private key for authentication.

allOf.description: Request to initiate a scan of an Insights account.

agentPoolID.description: The ID of the agent pool to use for scanning. If not specified, the default agent pool will be used.

ScanOptions.description: Configuration options for controlling scan behavior.

batchSize.description: The batch size for processing resources during the scan.

listConcurrency.description: The concurrency level for list operations during the scan.

readConcurrency.description: The concurrency level for read operations during the scan.

readTimeout.description: The timeout duration for read operations, as a Go duration string (e.g., '30s', '5m').

allOf.description: The status of an Insights account scan, including resource count.

accountName.description: The name of the insights account associated with this scan.

resourceCount.description: The number of resources discovered by this scan.

allOf.description: Response containing the scan status and the next scheduled scan time.

nextScan.description: The timestamp of the next scheduled scan, if any.

ScheduledAction.description: ScheduledAction describes the state of a scheduled action.

created.description: The timestamp when this scheduled action was created.

definition.description: The action definition, which varies based on the action kind.

id.description: The unique identifier for this scheduled action.

kind.description: The kind of action to be executed.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: The kind of scheduled action.
enumFieldComments: A scheduled deployment action.
A scheduled environment secret rotation action.
A scheduled Insights scan action.
An agent automation.

lastExecuted.description: The timestamp of the last execution, if any.

modified.description: The timestamp when this scheduled action was last modified.

nextExecution.description: The timestamp of the next scheduled execution.

orgID.description: The organization ID that owns this scheduled action.

paused.description: Whether the scheduled action is currently paused.

scheduleCron.description: A cron expression defining the recurring schedule.

scheduleOnce.description: A timestamp for a one-time scheduled execution.

ScheduledActionHistoryEvent.description: ScheduledActionHistoryEvent describes the state of a scheduled deployment history event.

executed.description: The timestamp when the action was executed.

id.description: The unique identifier for this history event.

scheduledActionID.description: The ID of the scheduled action this event belongs to.

version.description: The version of the scheduled action at the time of execution.

allOf.description: Request to update the scheduled Insights scan settings.

paused.description: Whether the scheduled scan is paused.

scheduleCron.description: The cron expression defining the scan schedule.

allOf.description: Response containing the current scheduled Insights scan settings.

paused.description: Whether the scheduled scan is paused.

scheduleCron.description: The cron expression defining the scan schedule.

SecretRotation.description: Represents the state of a single secret rotation within an environment.

environmentPath.description: The path of the secret being rotated in the environment.

errorMessage.description: An error message if the rotation failed.

id.description: The unique identifier for this secret rotation.

status.description: The current status of the rotation.

SecretRotationEvent.description: An event representing a secret rotation operation on an environment.

completed.description: The timestamp when the rotation completed.

created.description: The timestamp when this rotation event was created.

environmentId.description: The ID of the environment being rotated.

errorMessage.description: An error message if the rotation failed.

id.description: The unique identifier for this rotation event.

postRotationRevision.description: The environment revision after the rotation, if completed.

preRotationRevision.description: The environment revision before the rotation.

rotations.description: The individual secret rotations within this event.

scheduledActionID.description: The ID of the scheduled action that triggered this rotation, if any.

status.description: The current status of the rotation event.

userID.description: The ID of the user who initiated the rotation.

SecretValue.description: A SecretValue describes a secret value on the wire. The JSON representation is an object with a required 'secret' field containing the plaintext and an optional 'ciphertext' field containing the encrypted representation.

ciphertext.description: The encrypted representation of the secret value.

Service.description:
A service is a Pulumi construct that aggregates items and additional metadata
that can be accessed by the service's members.

created.description: the time the service was create

description.description: an optional description of the service

itemCountSummary.description: item types to their count within the service based on the current user's permissions

members.description: a list of members that have access to the service

organizationName.description: the name of the organization this service belongs to

properties.description: an optional list of properties set on the service

addedByUser.description: who added the item reference to the service - this will always be a user

cloudCount.description: how many Pulumi cloud measured primitives are within this item

created.description: timestamp of when the item was  created

lastUpdate.description: when did the last update occur to this item, if any

name.description: the name (including any namespacing) of the item

organizationName.description: the name of the organization this item belongs to

version.description: optional field if the item has versioning

ServiceItemUpdate.description: An update that the item may have in it's history.

message.description: the message to show alongside the timestamp

success.description: used to toggle a status icon for the update

timestamp.description: the timestamp of the last update

ServiceMember.description:
A service member is either a direct user or a indirect user representation
that allows them to access and mutate a service.

avatarUrl.description: an optional avatar URL for the member

name.description: the login name of the member within Pulumi

type.description: the type of the member (e.g. team / user)

ServiceProperty.description: A property that the service will show in it's metadata.

SetInsightAccountTagsRequest.description: Request to set tags on an Insights account.

tags.description: The tags to set on the Insights account.

SourceContext.description: SourceContext describes some source code, and how to obtain it.

git.description: Git-based source context for obtaining source code from a repository.

hg.description: Mercurial-based source context for obtaining source code from a repository.

template.description: Template-based source context for obtaining source code from a template URL.

SourceContextGit.description: Git-based source context for obtaining source code from a repository.

branch.description: The branch to use from the repository.

commit.description:
(optional) Commit is the hash of the commit to deploy. If used, HEAD will be in detached mode. This
is mutually exclusive with the Branch setting. Either value needs to be specified.

gitAuth.description:
(optional) GitAuth allows configuring git authentication options
There are 3 different authentication options:
  * SSH private key (and its optional password)
  * Personal access token
  * Basic auth username and password
Only one authentication mode will be considered if more than one option is specified,
with ssh private key/password preferred first, then personal access token, and finally
basic auth credentials.

repoDir.description:
(optional) RepoDir is the directory to work from in the project's source repository
where Pulumi.yaml is located. It is used in case Pulumi.yaml is not
in the project source root.

repoUrl.description: The URL of the git repository.

SourceContextGitRequest.description: Request to configure a git-based source context.

branch.description: The branch to use from the repository.

commit.description: The commit hash to deploy. Mutually exclusive with `branch`.

repoDir.description: The subdirectory within the repository where Pulumi.yaml is located.

repoUrl.description: The URL of the git repository.

SourceContextHg.description: Mercurial-based source context for obtaining source code from a repository.

branch.description: The branch to use from the repository.

hgAuth.description:
(optional) HgAuth allows configuring Mercurial authentication options.
There are 3 different authentication options:
  * SSH private key (and its optional password)
  * Personal access token
  * Basic auth username and password
Only one authentication mode will be considered if more than one option is specified,
with ssh private key/password preferred first, then personal access token, and finally
basic auth credentials.

repoDir.description:
(optional) RepoDir is the directory to work from in the project's source repository
where Pulumi.yaml is located. It is used in case Pulumi.yaml is not
in the project source root.

repoUrl.description: The URL of the Mercurial repository.

revision.description:
(optional) Revision is the changeset hash to check out. If used, the working directory
will be updated to this specific revision. This is mutually exclusive with the Branch setting.
Either value needs to be specified.

SourceContextHgRequest.description: Request to configure a Mercurial-based source context.

branch.description: The branch to use from the repository.

repoDir.description: The subdirectory within the repository where Pulumi.yaml is located.

repoUrl.description: The URL of the Mercurial repository.

revision.description: The changeset hash to check out.

SourceContextRequest.description: Request to configure a source context: git-based, Mercurial-based, or template-based. Only one may be specified.

SourceContextTemplate.description: Template-based source context for obtaining source code from a template URL.

gitAuth.description:
GitAuth allows configuring git authentication options
The only authentication option is personal access token as of now.

sourceUrl.description:
The URL of the template source. Supports two URL schemes:

**Registry-backed templates** use the `registry://` scheme with the format:
`registry://templates/source/publisher/name[@version]`

- `source`: The template source identifier (e.g., the registry source name)
- `publisher`: The organization or user that published the template
- `name`: The template name
- `version`: Optional semver version (e.g., `1.0.0`). If omitted, defaults to the latest version

Example: `registry://templates/pulumi/acme-corp/aws-vpc@2.1.0`

**VCS-backed templates** use standard VCS URLs (GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, etc.):
`https://github.com/org/repo`

SourceContextTemplateRequest.description: Request to configure a template-based source context.

gitAuth.description: Git authentication configuration for accessing the template source.

sourceUrl.description:
The URL of the template source. Supports two URL schemes:

**Registry-backed templates** use the `registry://` scheme with the format:
`registry://templates/source/publisher/name[@version]`

- `source`: The template source identifier (e.g., the registry source name)
- `publisher`: The organization or user that published the template
- `name`: The template name
- `version`: Optional semver version (e.g., `1.0.0`). If omitted, defaults to the latest version

Example: `registry://templates/pulumi/acme-corp/aws-vpc@2.1.0`

**VCS-backed templates** use standard VCS URLs (GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, etc.):
`https://github.com/org/repo`

StackDeploymentsMetadataResponse.description:
Metadata related to all of a stack's deployments. This is different from settings in that
it applies to all deployments with the same ProgramID; it's not configurable per deployment.

drift.description: Drift contains the drift status of the stack and associated metadata

hasSchedules.description: HasSchedules is true if the stack has at least one schedule.

organizationPaused.description: OrgPaused is true if all deployments are paused for the org.

paused.description:
Paused is true if new deployments for this stack right now would be paused. This could
be due to a stack-level or org-level pause.

stackPaused.description: StackPaused is true if deployments for this stack specifically are paused.

StackDriftStatus.description: The drift detection status for a stack.

driftDetected.description: Whether drift was detected in the latest run.

latestDriftRun.description: The identifier of the latest drift detection run.

runInProgress.description: Whether a drift detection run is currently in progress.

StackMetadata.description: StackMetadata returns metadata about a stack.

notificationSettings.description: The notification settings for this stack.

orgName.description: The organization that owns the stack.

ownedBy.description: The user with ownership of this stack

projectName.description: The project that contains the stack.

stackName.description: The stack name within the project.

StackNotificationSettings.description: StackNotificationSettings is the object containing the notification settings for a stack.

notifyUpdateFailure.description: Whether to send a notification when a stack update fails.

notifyUpdateSuccess.description: Whether to send a notification when a stack update succeeds.

StackOverviewResponse.description: StackOverviewResponse contains data to be displayed for the stack overview.

referencedStacks.description: The list of stacks referenced by this stack.

resources.description: The resources belonging to this stack.

tags.description: The tags associated with this stack.

StackReference.description: StackReference contains information about a stack reference.

name.description: The name of the referenced stack.

organization.description: The organization that owns the referenced stack.

routingProject.description: The project used for routing to the referenced stack.

version.description: The version of the stack when it was referenced.

StackTag.description: StackTag holds the name and value of a single stack tag.

name.description: The tag key, which must conform to the stack tag naming rules.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments:
StackTagName is the key for the tags bag in stack. This is just a string, but we use a type alias to provide a richer
description of how the string is used in our apitype definitions.
enumFieldComments: ProjectNameTag is a tag that represents the name of a project (coresponds to the `name` property of Pulumi.yaml).
ProjectRuntimeTag is a tag that represents the runtime of a project (the `runtime` property of Pulumi.yaml).
ProjectDescriptionTag is a tag that represents the description of a project (Pulumi.yaml's `description`).
ProjectTemplateTag is a tag that represents the template that was used to create a project.

VCSOwnerNameTag is a tag that represents the name of the owner on the cloud VCS that this stack
may be associated with (inferred by the CLI based on git remote info).

VCSRepositoryNameTag is a tag that represents the name of a repository on the cloud VCS that this stack
may be associated with (inferred by the CLI based on git remote info).

VCSRepositoryKindTag is a tag that represents the kind of the cloud VCS that this stack
may be associated with (inferred by the CLI based on the git remote info).

VCSRepositoryRootTag is a tag that represents the root directory of the repository on the cloud VCS that
this stack may be associated with (pulled from git by the CLI)

value.description: The tag value associated with this key.

StackTeam.description: StackTeam is a team that has access to a particular stack.

description.description: A description of the team.

displayName.description: The display name of the team.

isMember.description: IsMember is true if the requesting user is a member of the team.

name.description: The unique name of the team.

permission.description: The permission level this team has on the stack.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments:
StackPermission is a value describing the permission level a user has for a Pulumi stack.
Like OrganizationRole, there is a total ordering of StackPermission values, where each value
grants an additional layer of permissions. IMPORTANT: Do not rely on the numeric values for
auth checks, instead, just refer to these as opaque names. e.g. use explicit matching in a
case-statements instead of "if perm >= StackPermissionX". This prevents errors if we later
add a new StackPermission "in between" two existing ones, but with a new, higher value.
Previously, the StackPermission value was a series of bit flags, with each bit controlling
a specific operation on the stack. However, that proved difficult to describe and maintain.
So the newer, "fixed set" approach is used instead.
The conversion from the legacy permission values and the new StackPermission values is done
transparently, whenever data is read/written to the database. (Converting to the newer
values as needed.) We will only be able to remove these legacy values after we deploy the
code which can read both formats, and only writes the newer format.
enumFieldComments: StackPermissionNone provides no access.

StackPermissionRead provides read-only access, including the ability to
decrypt encrypted configuration secrets stored in the service.
StackPermissionWrite provides read/write access to a stack.

StackPermissionAdmin provides admin-level access to the stack. For example,
being able to run `pulumi destroy` or delete the stack.

StackPermissionCreator is an implementation quirk of how we mark the user who created
a stack. The value is not exported from this module, and is converted to StackPermissionAdmin
before being returned to callers. However, in a few places we want to handle the user who
created a stack a little differently, and this allows us to do so without exporting a separate
"StackPermissionAdminWhoAlsoHappenedToCreateTheStackToo" value.

time.description: The timestamp when the update was created.

StepRun.description: StepRun contains information about a step run.

lastUpdated.description: The timestamp when the step was last updated.

started.description: The timestamp when the step started.

status.description: The current status of the step.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: StepStatus describes the status of a step in a workflow job run.
enumFieldComments: StepStatusNotStarted indicates that a step has not yet started.
StepStatusRunning indicates that a step is running.
StepStatusFailed indicates that a step has failed.
StepStatusSucceeded indicates that a step has succeeded.

SubmitChangeRequestRequest.description: SubmitChangeRequestRequest is used to submit a draft change request for approval

description.description: Description/justification for the change request

TargetEntity.description: TargetEntity contains populated details about the targeted entity for a change request or gate

allOf.description: Environment entity information if the target is an environment

Team.description:
Team describes a Pulumi team. A Pulumi team may have its organization
managed by another service, such as GitHub.

accounts.description: The list of account permissions granted to the team.

description.description: A free-form text description of the team's purpose.

displayName.description: The human-readable display name shown in the UI.

environments.description: The list of environment settings for the team.

kind.description: The kind of team (e.g., pulumi or GitHub-backed).

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: TeamKind is the kind of team.
enumFieldComments: TeamKindGitHub is for teams where membership is managed by GitHub.
TeamKindPulumi is for teams where membership is managed by Pulumi.
TeamKindSCIM is for SCIM provisioned teams. Membership is managed by its IdP.

listMembersError.description:
ListMembersError is the error message if an error was encountered whilst trying to
contact the team's backend (eg. GitHub). The UI will only show this error if it is non-nil
and if Members itself is an empty slice.

name.description: The unique identifier name of the team within the organization.

roleIds.description:
RoleIDs are the IDs of the FGA roles assigned to the team, if any.
Currently only one role per team is supported.

stacks.description: The list of stack permissions granted to the team.

userRole.description: UserRole is the calling user's role on the given team.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: TeamRole is the kind of role a user has in a team.

TeamAccountPermission.description: TeamAccountPermission is the permission team membership grants to an account.

permission.description: The permission level the team has on this Insights account.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: InsightsAccountPermission is a value describing the permission level a user has for a Pulumi Insights account.
enumFieldComments: InsightsAccountPermissionNone provides no access.
InsightsAccountPermissionRead provides read-only access.
InsightsAccountPermissionWrite provides read/write access to an account.
InsightsAccountPermissionAdmin provides admin-level access to the account.

permissionSetName.description: Display name of the permission set for this account, when available. Enables read-only entity access UI without requiring RoleRead.

TeamEnvironmentSettings.description: TeamEnvironmentSettings contains additional settings a team applies to an environment.

maxOpenDuration.description: The maximum duration an environment session can remain open, as a Go duration string (e.g. "1h30m").

permission.description: The permission level the team has on this environment.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: EnvironmentPermission is an enum describing the permission level to access an environment.

permissionSetName.description: Display name of the permission set for this environment, when available. Enables read-only entity access UI without requiring RoleRead.

allOf.description: TeamMemberInfo is a UserInfo extended to contain the TeamRole.

role.description: The member's role within the team.

x-pulumi-model-property.enumComments: TeamRole is the kind of role a user has in a team.

TeamPermission.description: TeamPermission describes the team and the stack permission that team has for a specific stack.

permission.description: The permission level the team has on the stack (e.g., read, write, admin).
